title: The Wingman

jokes.text: The Crows Have Eyes: The Crowening
jokes.punchline: Awkward portmanteau combining 'crow' and '-ening' suffix
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay that establishes the absurd B-movie world

jokes.text: Hosts giggling and showing faux sympathy at crow attack footage
jokes.setup: Serious news story about an attack
jokes.punchline: Hosts barely containing their amusement at the ridiculous situation
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy showing the disconnect between serious news format and ridiculous content

dialogue.speaker: TV Host 1
dialogue.line: We're following a developing story out of the city where a man was attacked by a swan.

dialogue.speaker: TV Host 2
dialogue.line: The incident occurred at approximately 2 PM near the waterfront...

dialogue.speaker: TV Host 1
dialogue.line: Authorities say the swan was... unprovoked.

dialogue.speaker: TV Host 2
dialogue.line: The victim is expected to make a full recovery.

jokes.text: and one severed earlobe
jokes.setup: Moira downplaying injuries from the stunt
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions someone lost an earlobe
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira delivery - treating serious injury as minor detail with characteristic dramatic understatement

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm absolutely fine. A few bruises, nothing serious. You should see the other guy.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, actually, Derek lost an earlobe, but that's hardly my concern.

jokes.text: It is?! (Laughs excitedly)
jokes.setup: Moira acting like she knew about the success
jokes.punchline: Her genuine surprise breaks the facade
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Moira's vanity cracking to show genuine excitement

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, of course I knew it would be a smashing success. I have an impeccable eye for talent and entertainment value.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I simply chose to let others discover it organically, you understand.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Wait—it actually succeeded?

jokes.text: This whole PR coup d'état never would've happened without her
jokes.punchline: Using overly dramatic French phrase for a simple PR stunt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - turning simple credit into grandiose political metaphor

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Alexis, darling, your help with my public image has been simply divine.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Thanks, Mom. It was really just a simple social media post.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A simple post? Chérie, you have orchestrated a veritable 'tour de force' of rehabilitation! A true 'pièce de résistance!'

jokes.text: Poor dear's not mic'd
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to speak on TV
jokes.punchline: Moira dismissively explaining why we can't hear her
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke that works as both TV production humor and character moment

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, she's not miked.

jokes.text: I believe you've met my roommate, Bob
jokes.punchline: Revealing Bob is living with Ronnie after his divorce
jokes.explanation: Sets up the pathetic Bob situation effectively with Ronnie's bluntness

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: Yeah, so Bob's been staying with me since the divorce went through.

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: He's in the spare room. Keeps asking me if I need anything, it's driving me crazy.

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: I told him, 'Bob, you're not my wife anymore, you're just my roommate now.'

jokes.text: I don't mind a dirty room. I hope that doesn't sound as sad as... as it feels
jokes.punchline: His self-aware admission of how pathetic he sounds
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Bob's desperate state with painful self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: I'm very flexible. I can work around your schedule. I'm available evenings, weekends, holidays...

jokes.text: you should really hydrate after all that crying
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact acknowledgment of Bob's constant crying
jokes.explanation: Johnny's practical approach to Bob's emotional crisis is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Would you like some water, Bob?

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Yes, please.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'll just get you a glass. I know you'll need tissues for the tears.

jokes.text: Gwen used to love water. (Sobbing)
jokes.punchline: Bob finding a way to connect everything back to his ex-wife
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly pathetic - even basic elements trigger Bob's emotional spiral

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Water? Well, that reminds me of the time Sheila wanted to install a koi pond. Cost me thirty thousand dollars. Thirty thousand! She said it would be 'relaxing.' You know what was relaxing? Our divorce settlement.

jokes.text: That's not exactly how I'd describe our guests, Roland
jokes.punchline: Johnny's diplomatic pushback on the harsh but accurate description
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's business instincts vs. Roland's bluntness

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, I think 'sad people' might be a bit of a broad generalization. Perhaps we could say 'people going through a transitional period' or 'guests seeking a fresh start.'

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: They're sad people, Johnny.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, well... semantics.

jokes.text: First off, we now have electricity. (Laughs)
jokes.setup: Johnny commenting on how dating has changed
jokes.punchline: Roland's exaggerated timeline suggesting Johnny is ancient
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland - taking a jab at Johnny with absurd historical reference

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, when I was your age, we didn't have any of this swiping business. We just walked up to a woman and said, 'Hey, you look decent. Wanna grab a drink?' And if she didn't mace you, it was a yes.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Now you kids are on your little apps, matching with people. Back in my day, if a woman rejected you, at least you got to see the look of disgust on her face in real time.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You're telling me you need an algorithm to find love? When I was courting your mother, the only thing I needed was a winning smile and a willingness to lie about my job.

jokes.text: Uh, lucky Gwen, she has another one of her male cousins visiting. (Chuckles then sobs)
jokes.setup: Bob trying to be positive about his situation
jokes.punchline: His forced chuckle turning into sobs as reality hits
jokes.explanation: Perfect emotional whiplash - Bob's attempt at optimism immediately crashing into despair

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Well, you know, I'm trying to look on the bright side here. Things could be worse, right? Heh... heh... *voice breaking* ...hehehehe...

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: *sobbing* Oh my God, what am I doing? Everything is terrible.

jokes.text: it's sort of burning my hand a bit because I can't put it down on anything
jokes.punchline: Revealing the practical problem of having no furniture
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup showing the absurdity of their furniture situation

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: This is actually really good coffee.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, thank you, David. I'm doing my best with what we have.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, well, your best is surprisingly competent. Where did you get the beans?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The general store. I've been experimenting.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, it's exceptional. Though I do have one question — where exactly am I supposed to sit while drinking it?

jokes.text: our feet up on the... (Knock at the door) - You happy now?
jokes.setup: David about to mock the lack of coffee table
jokes.punchline: Perfect timing of the delivery interrupting his complaint
jokes.explanation: Great comedic timing and Patrick's smug satisfaction at being vindicated

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I was just about to say something cutting about the lack of a coffee table, but—

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, that's not nearly as satisfying.

jokes.text: Just do me a favour and watch these, and don't eat them. - Mm. No guarantees
jokes.setup: Patrick asking David to watch the pancakes
jokes.punchline: David's honest admission about his self-control
jokes.explanation: Good character moment establishing David's relationship with food

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Can you just watch the pancakes for a second?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I have no self-control.

jokes.text: Wow. We're still doing that
jokes.setup: Jake greeting David with air kisses
jokes.punchline: David's awkward acknowledgment of their past intimate greeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect David awkwardness in navigating past relationships in front of Patrick

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: Hey David, air kisses!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, well, that's... that's not necessary. We've moved past that.

jokes.text: It really brings out your lips. - Okay
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately sexual compliment that makes David uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly awkward flirtation that's both specific and uncomfortable

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: That sweater really hugs you in all the right places.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Okay, that's... that's inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: I'm just saying, it's a great fit.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I'm going to go now.

jokes.text: I am also wearing a sweater
jokes.punchline: Patrick's understated bid for attention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patrick jealousy delivered with characteristic understated delivery

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I got a haircut.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Nice.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I also learned to make hollandaise sauce from scratch.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's great.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: And I've been going to the gym.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Good for you.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I'm also fluent in French now.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wow.

jokes.text: A lot of sweat went into this one
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally sexual description of his work process
jokes.explanation: Jake's oblivious innuendo adds to the awkward dynamic

dialogue.speaker: David Rose
dialogue.line: I take great pride in my work. Every piece is handled with care, attention to detail, and a certain... firmness of grip.

dialogue.speaker: Moira Rose
dialogue.line: David, that's what she said.

jokes.text: you guys make a beautiful couple
jokes.setup: Jake hitting on David while Patrick is present
jokes.punchline: His disingenuous compliment to the couple he's trying to break up
jokes.explanation: Classic manipulative move disguised as kindness

jokes.text: your face is all red. It's beet red. - It is not! - Mm-hmm. It's flush
jokes.setup: David trying to act unaffected by Jake
jokes.punchline: Patrick pointing out his obvious physical reaction
jokes.explanation: Good relationship comedy - Patrick knows David better than David knows himself

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm fine. Completely fine. Jake doesn't affect me at all.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: David, you're literally sweating through your shirt.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's warm in here.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: We're outside.

jokes.text: It was a real 'Fyre Fest.' (Chuckles)
jokes.punchline: Reference to the disastrous music festival
jokes.explanation: Weak pop culture reference that doesn't fit Ted's character or add much comedy

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, the fire ants have completely taken over the town. It's like Folderol all over again.

jokes.text: Fresh omelettes
jokes.punchline: Alexis's completely missing the conservation point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - complete obliviousness to serious environmental issues

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're saying that turtle eggs are actually a delicacy? Because I would totally eat those.

jokes.text: The Daily Mail called it a 'flap for the ages!'
jokes.setup: Moira quoting praise for her performance
jokes.punchline: Bird-related pun in the review
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that fits both the crow theme and Moira's theatrical nature

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The review says, 'Ms. Rose's performance was absolutely soaring.' I'm a bird!

jokes.text: you look like a guy who had a million bucks, but then lost it. (Laughs)
jokes.setup: Johnny saying Bob looks like a million bucks
jokes.punchline: Roland's more accurate assessment that reflects their actual situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that works on multiple levels - describing both Bob and the Roses

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You look like a million bucks.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: A million bucks? More like a million bucks in debt.

jokes.text: How'd she end up with you?
jokes.setup: Johnny saying Moira liked funny men when single
jokes.punchline: Roland's devastating implication that Johnny isn't funny
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland burn - simple, effective, and character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, that explains a lot, doesn't it?

jokes.text: (High voice) Hello, I'm... I'm Gwen. - Oh...
jokes.setup: Johnny demonstrating how to approach women
jokes.punchline: Accidentally using Bob's ex-wife's name in the roleplay
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Johnny's thoughtless choice triggering Bob's trauma

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Okay, so you approach her like this... 'Hi, I'm Johnny Rose, I've made some unfortunate business decisions, but I have excellent posture.'

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: That's not really—

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, no, let me finish. Then you say, 'Sheila, I know we had our differences, but I think we could really make this work.'

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa—that's my ex-wife's name!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Is it? Well, that explains the look on your face.

jokes.text: I saw Bob standing there and that was the first name I thought of. They're always together
jokes.punchline: His logical but emotionally tone-deaf reasoning
jokes.explanation: Johnny's business logic completely missing the emotional impact

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I wanted something that would set him apart. Something memorable. David is a very common name, so I thought, 'Why not make it interesting?' I named him David because it's a solid, dependable name. Like a good investment portfolio.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: That's lovely, dear.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: The middle name, Rose, was your mother's idea. I wanted to honor that. Though I'll admit, I didn't think through how it would sound with the last name.

jokes.text: you're gonna be drowning in phone numbers
jokes.punchline: Absurdly optimistic prediction given Bob's current state
jokes.explanation: Johnny's misguided optimism contrasting with Bob's obvious distress

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Bob, listen to me. I can see it now. You're going to be very successful. Very successful indeed.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes. In about ten years, you're going to own your own business.

jokes.text: this coffee table really brings out your lips
jokes.punchline: Mocking Jake's earlier inappropriate compliment
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Patrick's jealousy disguised as humor

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: You know, this is a really nice coffee table.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Thank you. It's reclaimed wood from a 19th-century French château.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: It really brings out the color of your eyes.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: ...Did you just compliment my eyes by referencing furniture?

jokes.text: Jake is nobody's ex and everybody's ex
jokes.punchline: Paradoxical description of Jake's promiscuous nature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant paradox that perfectly captures Jake's sexual availability and emotional unavailability

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Jake is in a relationship, but he's also seeing other people. It's very confusing.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: He's simultaneously committed and completely unavailable.

jokes.text: I always have the option of online shopping and just putting things in the cart. That's not gonna happen
jokes.punchline: David's self-aware analogy about temptation and self-control
jokes.explanation: Perfect David metaphor - using consumer behavior to explain sexual temptation

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Look, I understand the appeal, but I have to exercise some self-control here. It's like... I'm Odysseus, and temptation is the siren song, and I am simply choosing to tie myself to the mast.

jokes.text: The competition is eating crow
jokes.punchline: Crow-themed pun about their success
jokes.explanation: Adequate pun that maintains the crow theme but lacks originality

jokes.text: Not those - those are from Joyce Dewitt
jokes.setup: Indicating which flowers are for Alexis
jokes.punchline: Casual name-drop of obscure TV actress from the 70s
jokes.explanation: Good Moira moment - random celebrity connection that suggests her extensive industry network

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: These are for Alexis. She's going through a phase where she only accepts flowers that have been hand-selected by someone with impeccable taste and a working knowledge of obscure 1970s television actresses.

jokes.text: I haven't received flowers from someone I wasn't... friends with
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining her confusion about the flowers
jokes.punchline: Her euphemistic way of saying she hasn't received professional flowers before
jokes.explanation: Good Alexis character moment - her romantic history interfering with professional recognition

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I just... I've never gotten flowers from a professional before.

jokes.text: they'll likely be dead in a week
jokes.punchline: Moira's matter-of-fact dismissal with unintended metaphor for career
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that works literally and metaphorically for Alexis's career dilemma

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm so worried about the flowers. What if they don't survive the trip?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, some flowers are simply meant to wilt.

jokes.text: What other hand? - Ted!
jokes.setup: Moira presenting only one side of Alexis's dilemma
jokes.punchline: Alexis having to remind her mother about her boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira selfishness - completely forgetting about Ted in her excitement about success

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, darling, you simply cannot pass up an opportunity like this. A chance to expand your horizons, to reinvent yourself in a new city. It's what I did, and look at me now.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Mom, I have a boyfriend.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, well, these things are fleeting, aren't they?

jokes.text: What're the odds of... running into my marriage counselor here?
jokes.setup: Bob seeming to have success at the bar
jokes.punchline: Revealing his 'pickup' was actually his therapist
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - what looked like dating success is actually another professional rejection

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Things are really turning around for me at the bar. I've been working on my confidence, my social skills...

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: In fact, I picked someone up last night.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You did?

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Yes. We went back to her place, we talked for hours...

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Bob, that's amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Well, it turns out it was my therapist. She was just... listening to me.

jokes.text: she's chosen to go with Gwen
jokes.punchline: Even his therapist is choosing his ex-wife over him
jokes.explanation: Devastating escalation - Bob can't even keep professional relationships

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Even my therapist is choosing my ex-wife over me.

jokes.text: You said they wouldn't ask
jokes.setup: Woman asking what he said that was funny
jokes.punchline: Bob's panicked reference to Johnny's earlier assurance
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how the plan immediately falls apart

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: What did you say that was funny?

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: I... Johnny said I was funny earlier!

jokes.text: Well, next time, observe someone else
jokes.setup: Johnny explaining it was Bob's observation
jokes.punchline: Woman's cutting dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good sharp putdown that completely deflates their scheme

dialogue.speaker: Johnny Rose
dialogue.line: It was actually Bob's observation.

dialogue.speaker: Woman at bar
dialogue.line: Well, Bob's an idiot.

jokes.text: Crrrash and burn, Johnny
jokes.punchline: Roland's sound effect commentary
jokes.explanation: Roland's childish delight in Johnny's failure fits his character

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You miss 100% of the shots you don't take. Wayne Gretzky. Michael Scott.

jokes.text: But she was a dead ringer for Gwen
jokes.punchline: Everything reminds him of his ex-wife
jokes.explanation: Shows how Bob's trauma interferes with any potential new relationship

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Everything reminds me of my ex-wife.

jokes.text: Is it new? And does it come in an adult size?
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment implying the shirt is too small
jokes.explanation: Classic David snark covering his nervousness about the evening

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That shirt really... hugs you in all the right places.

jokes.text: This is a leather-scented body moisturizer
jokes.punchline: David's overly specific and pretentious grooming routine
jokes.explanation: Perfect David specificity about his beauty routine

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: So what cologne do you wear?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I don't wear cologne. I use a custom scent that's been developed specifically for my pH balance and natural musk. It's a blend of Bulgarian rose, Tahitian vanilla, and a hint of vetiver that I have imported quarterly from a small apothecary in Grasse.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Right. So... cologne.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No. It's a *bespoke fragrance experience*. I also have a separate morning and evening iteration, depending on my circadian rhythm and the ambient humidity levels.

jokes.text: Go long, go short. Just pick one
jokes.punchline: David's exasperation with Patrick's fashion indecision
jokes.explanation: David's fashion expertise clashing with Patrick's practicality

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Patrick, the sleeves are fine. They're a quarter inch shorter than the other jacket.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: But is that intentional?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Does it matter? It's a shirt.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: It matters to me.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, it shouldn't. You look fine. You look better than fine, you look great. Why do you do this?

jokes.text: A lot of people are here for drinks, David
jokes.punchline: Patrick's understated reaction to what's clearly a party/orgy setup
jokes.explanation: Patrick's innocent observation of what's obviously not just drinks

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Well, this is... a lot of people in a very small space.

jokes.text: ours is um... kosher
jokes.setup: Needing to get wine from the car
jokes.punchline: Patrick's absurd excuse to leave
jokes.explanation: Patrick's panic leading to completely nonsensical excuse

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I need to go to the car.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I left my emotional support wine out there.

jokes.text: We're gonna start the massage circle soon
jokes.punchline: Revealing the evening's planned sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward escalation that confirms their worst fears

dialogue.speaker: David Rose
dialogue.line: Well, I've planned a very special evening for us.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick Brewer
dialogue.line: Oh? What did you have in mind?

dialogue.speaker: David Rose
dialogue.line: I was thinking we could... reorganize the bedroom closet.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick Brewer
dialogue.line: David, that's the least sexy thing you've ever said to me.

dialogue.speaker: David Rose
dialogue.line: I know. Isn't it thrilling?

jokes.text: Can I not have one thing for myself?
jokes.setup: Finding David and Patrick at Jake's party
jokes.punchline: Stevie's exasperation at their interference in her fun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie - wanting to enjoy the party without their judgment

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Oh my God, what are you two doing here? I'm trying to have fun for once in my life.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: We're just... we came to support you.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Well, you're not supporting me. You're suffocating me. There's a difference.

jokes.text: Fresh omelettes
jokes.punchline: Alexis still completely missing the conservation point
jokes.explanation: Weaker callback that doesn't add much beyond establishing Alexis's consistent obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: The turtle eggs are really important for conservation.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, Ted, I don't care about the eggs. I just think it's so sweet that you're protecting them.

jokes.text: Well, I put some screens on the windows. I wouldn't really call it a renovation
jokes.punchline: Ted's modest correction of her exaggeration
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Ted's practicality vs Alexis's tendency to dramatize

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Ted, your apartment renovation is amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, I just painted the walls and replaced some outlets.

jokes.text: the screens that I installed are actually attracting more bugs somehow
jokes.setup: Ted trying to improve his living situation
jokes.punchline: His home improvements backfiring spectacularly
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing how Ted's practical nature sometimes works against him

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I've decided to take matters into my own hands and really spruce up my place.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I'm going to paint the walls, maybe install some new shelving.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: How hard could it be?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, turns out I'm not a contractor. I'm a veterinarian.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: My walls now look like a Jackson Pollock painting, if Jackson Pollock hated aesthetics.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: The shelving fell down and took out my TV.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: And I'm pretty sure there's a gas leak now.

jokes.text: Would've saved us some time
jokes.punchline: Alexis's blunt assessment that this would have been a dealbreaker
jokes.explanation: Good Alexis moment - her honesty about her lifestyle requirements

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: A shared bathroom? That would have been a dealbreaker for me.

jokes.text: the ants are back and, uh, I think what we're about to do probably wouldn't end very well if a fire ant was involved
jokes.punchline: Fire ants interrupting their romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with awkward timing

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Oh, this is nice. Finally, we get some private time together.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know, I've been looking forward to this all day.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: So, should we...?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Yes, let's just—

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Ow! Ow! Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What? What happened?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Fire ants! There are fire ants all over me!

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I don't know how they got here but—ow, ow, ow—this is not romantic!

jokes.text: I was really hoping to not talk shop
jokes.setup: Being encouraged to use his car expertise
jokes.punchline: Bob wanting to avoid the one thing he's good at
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bob's misguided attempt to be someone he's not

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: I appreciate the vote of confidence, but I really don't want to get into the car business.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Why not? You clearly know what you're doing.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: That's exactly why I don't want to do it. I've spent my entire life avoiding the one thing I'm actually competent at.

jokes.text: that was probably because women used to close out their tabs and go home when you came into the bar
jokes.punchline: Roland's alternative interpretation of the nickname
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that completely reframes Johnny's boast into an insult

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: A closer? I thought you said you were a 'clozer,' like someone who closes their eyes a lot.

jokes.text: Which is what the women used to say to each other when they saw you walking through the door
jokes.punchline: Roland doubling down on the same insult structure
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the same joke pattern

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, I'd close out my tab, but I'm not sure the account can handle that kind of emotional trauma.

jokes.text: Which is that what your matchmaker said!
jokes.setup: Johnny saying he won't set Roland up anymore
jokes.punchline: Roland continuing the pattern one more time
jokes.explanation: Third iteration of the same joke - diminishing returns but Roland's persistence is funny

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm not setting you up with anyone else, Roland. I'm done.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh come on, Johnny. One more time. What about that woman at the real estate office? The one with the... you know, the face?

jokes.text: Who is she and how do we get a mouth full of her?
jokes.punchline: Moira's unintentionally sexual description of wine tasting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - her dramatic language creating unintended sexual innuendo

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The wine tasting notes describe a full-bodied experience with a long, lingering finish that coats the palate and leaves you completely satisfied.

tmdb_overview: Alexis and Moira deal with the aftermath of a viral video, while Johnny helps Bob get back out in the dating world.
insight: Cringe-driven character comedy sustains a below-average episode with minimal standout moments.
